# 2026-03-27

## Morning Idea (auto-generated)
- **Topic:** Collapsible sections for Groups page
- **Angle:** UX pattern from another product (Airtable/Notion)
- **Idea:** Split Manual vs Default groups into collapsible sections with count badges instead of flat paginated list. Solves pagination awkwardness (29 items at 25/page) and makes the Manual/Default hierarchy structural.
- **Relates to:** Groups page audit from 2026-03-25

## Xero Diff — Per Transaction Settings (2026-03-27)

### What we did
- Logged into new Xero test org (`Xero test`) on demo.synderapp.com
- Scraped all Per Transaction Settings tabs for Xero
- Added **Xero Diff** tab to `reports/per-transaction-audit.html`

### Key differences found (Xero vs QBO)

**Tab structure:** Xero has 8 tabs (vs 11 in QBO). Missing: Application Fees, Expenses, Multicurrency.

**General:** Xero adds `Auto-reconcile Xero transactions`. QBO-only: Apply location, Sync payments without invoices as, Balance Reserve account.

**Sales:** Xero adds: Generic customer name field, Gift cards settings (Gift Card Liability Account), Discounts settings (Sync discounts as products), Sync only Payments/Deposits. QBO-only: Payment Method, Posting date, Enable QuickBooks Doc Numbers, Customer name search priority, Applied Balance Account. Label changed: "Apply generic customer" (QBO) → "Generic customer" (Xero).

**Invoices:** Xero adds: "Sync sales transactions as Invoices". Label changed: "Sync unpaid (open) invoices" → "Sync unpaid Shopify orders" (shorter description too). QBO-only: Sync zero invoices.

**Products/Services:** Xero drops product type selector (Non-inventory/Service/Inventory) — uses sales + purchase accounts only. Wording: "should be" → "should have". Xero adds Inventory settings section (Inventory management toggle).

**Taxes:** Xero drops Default tax code dropdown. Apply Taxes has no description (tooltip only). Generic tax code is much richer in Xero (conditional logic for Contains taxes / Does not contain taxes, shipping). Xero adds: Expense tax settings section, Marketplace Facilitator Tax Line Account, Track marketplace facilitator tax as your own payable, Retail Delivery Fee Payable Account.

**Fees:** "Vendor" → "Fee supplier"; "Category" → "Fee line account". Descriptions change: references QuickBooks Vendor → Xero Contact.

**Payouts:** Same Yes/No bug on both. QBO-only: Transfer Funds To dropdown.

## QB Books Preview Prototype (2026-03-27)

Built a full two-tab interactive prototype for the "Books Preview" concept — shows users how their Synder data would look in QuickBooks before syncing.

### What was built
- `synder-settings.html` — faithful copy of Synder per-transaction settings page (Stripe / mzkt.by org), with conditional fields that reveal on toggle (generic customer name picker, fee accounts, tax settings, archive days, etc.)
- `qb-preview-v2.html` — standalone QB Online UI clone with 6 views: All Sales, Journal Entry, Chart of Accounts, Banking, P&L, Balance Sheet + Dashboard
- Two-tab workflow: settings in one tab, QB preview in another. BroadcastChannel for real-time sync + localStorage for persistence across tab opens.
- Amber "changed" highlight system — shows exactly which rows/accounts change when a setting is modified
- "Preview in Books" button opens QB tab directly from settings page

### Data
- 10 Stripe transactions (Jan–Mar 2026): Pamela Anderson subscriptions, invoice payments, payouts, refunds, misc charges
- Matching real demo org structure (mzkt.by / Stripe, per-transaction mode)

### Settings that affect preview
Clearing account, income account, fee account, bank account, generic customer (+ name picker), track fees toggle, include taxes toggle, default product

### Published to GitHub
- Repo: `dashasyn/synder-prototypes`
- Path: `qb-preview/settings.html` + `qb-preview/preview.html`
- Added card to `prototypes/index.html`
- GitHub Pages URL: `https://dashasyn.github.io/synder-prototypes/qb-preview/settings.html`

### Notes
- QBO sandbox (rurururururururururururu23@gmail.com) requires MFA — couldn't scrape live UI; built from screenshots + knowledge
- Synder accounting API uses separate Spring auth token — direct API access blocked; used realistic hardcoded transaction data
- Prototype purpose: internal team presentation/discussion
